steps.title: Identify what earlier snow must explain
steps.content: The researchers concluded that net CO₂ increases when snow arrives earlier relative to the onset of soil surface freezing. For a finding to support this, it must explain the mechanism underlying this conclusion—why would earlier snow lead to increased net CO₂?

steps.title: Apply the mechanism to the choices
steps.content:
If snow arrives before soil surface freezing, it insulates the soil and maintains conditions warm enough for heterotrophic respiration (CO₂ released by soil microorganisms) to continue. But O. secunda and other plants remain dormant during subfreezing periods and cannot grow to absorb CO₂. Choice B directly identifies this dynamic: snow insulation enables respiration to continue when plants are typically not growing.
Choice A contradicts the mechanism by lowering respiration, C addresses the relative strength of effects rather than the arrival-timing mechanism, and D focuses on where snow persists rather than when it arrives.
This directly supports the conclusion: early snow's insulation sustains respiration during a period when plants cannot offset it through growth, resulting in a net increase in CO₂.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
